explanation: The input specifies that if 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, each individual step of the self-test can be skipped or rerun. Among the available atomic propositions, 'resumeVentilation' signifies that this selection was made, and 'testOxygenSensorSkip', 'testLeaksSkip', 'testAlarmsSkip', 'testPowerSwitchSkip', and 'testFl2Skip' (based on the atomic prop list) represent the ability to skip the main self-test steps (oxygen sensor, leaks, alarms, power switch, Fl2 flow meter). Thus, whenever 'resumeVentilation' is true, it should always be possible to perform any combination of these skip operations, meaning that the system permits these (optionally) at all times after the selection. The best-fit LTL template is 'G (bool_exp1 -> (bool_exp3 V (! bool_exp4)))', expressing that globally, after 'resumeVentilation', any valid skip-step (as an example, testOxygenSensorSkip) is permitted, and that it is not required for any specific rerun (i.e., reruns are optional, but skipping is always possible). For generality/clarity, we focus on a representative skip action. N_DURATION is not needed for this template. (For a fully comprehensive model you would use combinations of all skip props, but this shows the expected logical form.)

The natural language states that if 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, every step of the self-test procedure FUN.6 can be skipped or optionally rerun individually. The atomic propositions include individual skips for self-test steps (e.g., testOxygenSensorSkip, testLeaksSkip, testPowerSwitchSkip, testPSExpSkip, testFl2Skip, testAlarmsSkip). The most direct encoding is: whenever 'resumeVentilation' is true, all these skip propositions are permitted (true). In LTL, this can be modeled by saying that globally, if 'resumeVentilation' holds, then it implies any (typically a disjunction, but here more aptly a conjunction for all) of the self-test steps can be skipped (i.e., the system allows any or all skip steps to occur). This corresponds to template 30: 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', with bool_exp1 = 'resumeVentilation', and bool_exp3 = (testOxygenSensorSkip & testLeaksSkip & testPowerSwitchSkip & testPSExpSkip & testFl2Skip & testAlarmsSkip). N_DURATION is not required for this template.

The requirement states that if 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, every step of the self-test procedure FUN.6 can be skipped, or optionally rerun individually. Based on the atomic propositions, we interpret that when 'resumeVentilation' is true, the various self-test checks can be skipped—meaning the skip flags for the self-tests are permitted to be true. As examples, these include 'testOxygenSensorSkip', 'testLeaksSkip', 'testPowerSwitchSkip', 'testAlarmsSkip', 'testFl2Skip', and 'testPSExpSkip'. While the atomic propositions list these individually, the structure 'every step can be skipped' is best captured by stating that when 'resumeVentilation' is selected, all the skip flags can be true. More generally (with the given APs), this is expressed by:

G (resumeVentilation -> (testOxygenSensorSkip & testLeaksSkip & testPowerSwitchSkip & testAlarmsSkip & testFl2Skip & testPSExpSkip))

This LTL formula ensures that whenever 'resumeVentilation' is true (i.e., Resume Ventilation is selected), then all the skip flags for the individual self-test components are also true—meaning those checks can be skipped, exactly as desired. Optional rerun is permitted because this formula does not require future executions, only allows skips when 'Resume Ventilation' is selected. This captures the intent of the natural language input.
bool_exp3: testOxygenSensorSkip & testLeaksSkip & testPowerSwitchSkip & testAlarmsSkip & testFl2Skip & testPSExpSkip

explanation: The natural language states that if the 'Resume Ventilation' option is selected, any step of the self-test procedure (represented here by specific skip indicators such as 'testOxygenSensorSkip', 'testLeaksSkip', 'testPowerSwitchSkip', 'testFl2Skip', 'testPSExpSkip', 'testAlarmsSkip') can be skipped, or rerun. Among available atomic propositions, only the 'skip' indicators directly represent the ability to skip self-test steps. Thus, the property states that globally, if 'resumeVentilation' is activated, then all self-test skip indicators can be true (i.e., skipping is allowed); so, we use a conjunction of all these to capture the set of skippable steps. Optional rerun is a permission and not a requirement, so no explicit guarantee is needed in LTL for rerun, only that skipping is enabled. This is translated as: 'G (resumeVentilation -> (testOxygenSensorSkip & testLeaksSkip & testPowerSwitchSkip & testFl2Skip & testPSExpSkip & testAlarmsSkip))'. Chosen template 30 suits this, encoding an implication conditioned globally.

The requirement specifies that if 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, the steps of the self-test (in this context, exemplified by oxygen sensor, leaks, power switch, Fl2 flow meter, expiratory valve, and alarms) can be skipped or rerun individually. Each self-test step has a corresponding 'skip' atomic proposition (e.g., testOxygenSensorSkip) that, if set to true, indicates skipping that step. 

This property is captured using the LTL template: 'G (bool_exp1 -> (bool_exp4 V (bool_exp3 | bool_exp4)))', which enforces for all time steps that, if resumeVentilation is selected, for each self-test step at least (a) the step is skipped, or (b) the step is run and completes/repeats (which could be encoded as not skipped).

Here, we construct the condition such that if resumeVentilation is true, then for each self-test step (using an example of testOxygenSensorSkip), that step can either be skipped (testOxygenSensorSkip) or not skipped (!testOxygenSensorSkip), i.e., it may either be skipped or rerun optionally and independently (the 'or' clause allows this choice for each), and this pattern is generalizable for all such steps. For demonstration, testOxygenSensorSkip is used as the representative atomic proposition; in implementation, this would be replicated or extended over all relevant skip-test propositions. N_DURATION is not required for this template.

The requirement states that when 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, every step of the self-test procedure (for example, oxygen sensor test, leaks test, expiratory valve, power switch, Fl2 flow meter, and local alarms) can be skipped. This can be succinctly formalized as: If 'resumeVentilation' is true, then it is allowed that each of the self-test steps—testOxygenSensorSkip, testLeaksSkip, testPSExpSkip, testPowerSwitchSkip, testFl2Skip, testAlarmsSkip—can be set (i.e., skipped). Thus, we use a formula that universally expresses that when 'resumeVentilation' holds, all these skips may occur. This is modeled as a safety property and best mapped to 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)' (Template 30), where bool_exp1 is 'resumeVentilation', and bool_exp3 is the conjunction that all relevant self-test steps are either skipped or not required to run (i.e., all ...Skip flags are true, or the user may rerun them individually). Since 'skipping' is the allowed action upon 'Resume Ventilation', we use the option of all skips. Individual reruns are optional (not enforced by the formula). N_DURATION is not relevant to this always-immediate property, so it's unused (null).

The natural language requirement specifies that when 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, each step of the self-test procedure (represented by the various test skips for the oxygen sensor, leaks, power switch, Fl2, alarms, and expiratory valve) can be skipped or rerun individually. Since skipping these tests is enabled conditionally on 'Resume Ventilation', the property should guarantee that whenever 'resumeVentilation' is true, any of the step-skipping indicators can hold (i.e., steps may be skipped). This is best captured by a template expressing that globally, if 'resumeVentilation' holds, then the ability to skip any self-test step holds as well, using a disjunction of the skip indicators. The most fitting template is: 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)'.
bool_exp3: testOxygenSensorSkip | testLeaksSkip | testPowerSwitchSkip | testFl2Skip | testAlarmsSkip | testPSExpSkip

explanation: The input requires that whenever the 'Resume Ventilation' option is selected, any self-test step in the self-test procedure FUN.6 can be skipped (or optionally rerun). Among the provided atomic propositions, 'resumeVentilation' indicates Resume Ventilation is selected, and self-test steps that can be skipped are represented by specific skip flags such as 'testOxygenSensorSkip', 'testLeaksSkip', 'testPSExpSkip', 'testPowerSwitchSkip', 'testAlarmsSkip', 'testFl2Skip'. To capture that each one of these skip options is enabled (i.e., can be set) whenever 'resumeVentilation' is selected, we can express that globally, if 'resumeVentilation' is true, then any skip flag (or all skip flags, for a strict read) can be enabled. Since we need to express that every skip is allowed, we combine all skips using 'and' and connect to 'resumeVentilation' with implication. So, the property reads: Globally, if 'resumeVentilation' is true, then all self-test skip options are true (can be chosen) at the same moment. This is best modeled by template 30: 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', with bool_exp1 = resumeVentilation and bool_exp3 = (testOxygenSensorSkip & testLeaksSkip & testPSExpSkip & testPowerSwitchSkip & testAlarmsSkip & testFl2Skip). N_DURATION is not needed for this template.

The natural language specifies: 'If Resume Ventilation is selected, every step of the self-test procedure FUN.6 can be skipped or optionally rerun individually.' Since the atomic propositions for skipping self-test steps are: testOxygenSensorSkip, testLeaksSkip, testPSExpSkip, testPowerSwitchSkip, testFl2Skip, and testAlarmsSkip, the property to express is: Whenever resumeVentilation is true, then it is possible (i.e., allowed at every point) to skip any of these self-test steps. This is naturally captured by stating that whenever resumeVentilation holds, each of the skip propositions is always possibly true (since the scenario describes the situation as relaxing the constraints so a skip is always allowed after resume). The most direct LTL is to say that globally, if resumeVentilation is true, then globally (i.e., at every future time), any of the skip conditions could be true (using the or operator among all skip steps). So we use template 37: G (bool_exp1 -> (G bool_exp3)), where bool_exp1 = resumeVentilation, and bool_exp3 = testOxygenSensorSkip | testLeaksSkip | testPSExpSkip | testPowerSwitchSkip | testFl2Skip | testAlarmsSkip. N_DURATION is not used. This LTL says: Whenever Resume Ventilation is selected, from that point on, you are always allowed to skip any of the self-test steps (which enables skip or optionally rerun as rerun is not forbidden).
bool_exp3: testOxygenSensorSkip | testLeaksSkip | testPSExpSkip | testPowerSwitchSkip | testFl2Skip | testAlarmsSkip

explanation: The requirement is that after 'Resume Ventilation' is selected (resumeVentilation), every self-test step (represented as testOxygenSensorSkip, testLeaksSkip, testPowerSwitchSkip, testFl2Skip, and testAlarmsSkip) can be individually skipped or rerun. The most direct interpretation with the available templates is to express that, for each step, it is globally the case that if resumeVentilation is active, then it is possible (eventually, within a short time window) to activate the skip for that step. Since the [F[0,N_DURATION] ...] expressions are not, by themselves, boolean, but the LTL template expects 'bool_exp3' to be a boolean expression, we must use a disjunction of the atomic propositions indicating that any skip action is possible at any valid time. This can be expressed as: testOxygenSensorSkip | testLeaksSkip | testPowerSwitchSkip | testFl2Skip | testAlarmsSkip. This asserts that, whenever resumeVentilation is selected, at any time, at least one skip step can be active, which closely matches the intent that all can be optionally skipped or rerun. We use N_DURATION=2 for completeness.
